rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical PhI/TsOH-catalyzed oxidative cyclization of N-allylamides to dihydrooxazoles (and α-tosyloxylation of ketones) via an electrogenerated hypervalent iodine(III) Koser's reagent
electrochemical_role: Anodic 2e- oxidation of PhI (iodobenzene) in HFIP generates a hypervalent iodine(III) species [PhI(OCH(CF3)2)2] that acts as the active oxidant; iodine cycles between I(I) and I(III) at the anode

key_intermediates.name: PhI(OCH(CF3)2)2 (anodically generated hypervalent iodine(III) bis-HFIP-oxy species)
Koser's reagent PhI(OH)(OTs) after ligand exchange with TsOH·H2O
Iodonium-activated alkene adduct (III): tosyloxy-iodane bound to alkene with amide oxygen poised for cyclization

electron_flow: Anode removes 2e- from PhI in HFIP to form a PhI(III) bis-alkoxide; ligand exchange with TsOH·H2O gives Koser's reagent, which electrophilically activates the allyl double bond of the N-allylamide; the amide oxygen performs intramolecular 5-exo cyclization onto the iodonium-activated carbon, and reductive elimination from the resulting C-I(III) intermediate delivers OTs to the methylene and regenerates PhI.

probe_reagents.name: control without acid (TsOH·H2O omitted)
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction; starting material recovered, showing acid is required to form/stabilize Koser-type I(III) species

probe_reagents.name: control without PhI catalyst or without electricity
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction in α-tosyloxylation; confirms iodoarene mediator and anodic current are both essential

probe_reagents.name: TfOH as acid
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed (entry 1), attributed to instability of generated PhI(III) species

probe_reagents.name: BF3·Et2O as acid
probe_reagents.observation: Degradation of starting material, no desired product (entry 2)

probe_reagents.name: Gram-scale and catalyst recovery experiment
probe_reagents.observation: PhI recovered after 10 mmol scale-up (87% yield of 2a), supporting catalytic I(I)/I(III) turnover

notes: Mechanism (Scheme 4) is proposed by analogy to established PhI/Koser's-reagent chemistry and prior electrochemical hypervalent-iodine reports; no radical traps, KIE, EPR, CV, or DFT presented. The same catalytic system also performs α-tosyloxylation of ketones (Scheme 3) and 6-exo cyclization of homoallyl amides to dihydro-2H-pyrans, consistent with a generalized electrogenerated ArI(III) oxidant rather than a substrate-specific pathway. HFIP is implicated both as solvent stabilizing the cationic I(III) intermediate and as the initial alkoxide ligand on iodine.
